free_text: A study of 31,32,33P by the use of (alpha,p) reactions
on silicon isotopes
Radial Ridge cyclotron.
A 25 MeV beam of alpha particles.
The self-supporting targets were manufactured by
resistive evaporation of SiO2 to a thickness of
approximately 100 ug/cm2. Natural silicon dioxide was
used for the reactions on 28Si and silicon dioxide
enriched with 29Si or 30Si was used for the reaction
on those isotopes. The target thicknesses from the
silicon component were calculated from elastic
scattering data at 12.5 degr.
Pairs of solid state detectors formed
deltaE-E telescopes, four of which were placed
5 degr. apart on each of two arms in the scattering
chamber.
These detectors were used for particle
identification in conjunction with an analogue
multiplier.
The zero-range DWBA calculations were performed.
No information about source of uncertainty
Uncertainty in target thickness
